etymology.description: Depicts wheat: line in the middle (ear) + upward-facing lines (leaves) + downward-facing lines (stem and roots) -> borrowed for 'to come'
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted wheat, with elements representing the ear, leaves, stem, and roots of the plant. It was borrowed for its phonetic sound to mean 'to come', as these concepts involved growth and arrival metaphorically. Attempts to add semantic components like 止(止) (zhǐ, 'foot') or 辵(辵) (chuò, 'to walk') were made to clarify distinctions, but did not persist.

examples.chinese: 他从美国回来(他從美國回來)。
examples.english: He returned from the United States.

examples.chinese: 每当下雨(每當下雨)，他就会来接我(他就會來接我)。
examples.pinyin: Měi dāng xiàyǔ, tā jiù huì lái jiē wǒ.
examples.english: Whenever it rains, he will come to pick me up.
